_comment: Opinionated prefix-caching benchmark scenarios run by --tools aiperf --prefix-cache.
Each scenario produces multiple runs (cartesian product of concurrencies x arrivals x isl_profiles).
Presets:
- ci            : tiny regression sweep.
- full          : comprehensive serving-validation sweep.
- highcache_50k : customer trillion-scale shape -- 50K shared (cacheable) prefix
+ 5K new ISL + 500 OSL at concurrency 32. Steady-state KV-cache
hit-rate target ~90.9% = 50000/(50000+5000). Pairs shared_system
(100% reuse) with a matched zero-prefix baseline so the report
isolates the TTFT (P50/P90/P99) uplift from prefix caching.
Scenarios:
- shared_system  : single shared system prompt (100% prefix reuse).
- prefix_pool    : pool of N distinct prefixes; reuse_ratio = request_count / N.
- multi_turn     : multi-turn conversations re-sending history (organic reuse).
- mooncake_trace : trace-driven via AIPerf's --custom-dataset-type mooncake-trace.
Replays a JSONL trace with optional --synthesis-* multipliers
(https://github.com/ai-dynamo/aiperf/blob/main/docs/tutorials/prefix-synthesis.md).
- baseline       : zero-prefix control matched to shared_system/prefix_pool grid.
Arrival patterns (aiperf 0.5.0 --arrival-pattern choices):
- constant | poisson | gamma   (bursty traffic = gamma + smoothness<1)
Field reference:
isl_profiles[*].isl_mean / isl_stddev   : synthetic-input-tokens-mean / -stddev
osl_mean / osl_stddev                    : output-tokens-mean / -stddev
concurrencies                            : list of concurrency values to sweep
arrival_patterns[*].pattern              : constant | poisson | gamma
arrival_patterns[*].smoothness           : gamma shape (<1.0 = bursty)
request_count                            : total requests per run
shared_system_prompt_length              : tokens (mutually exclusive with prefix pool)
num_prefix_prompts / prefix_prompt_length: prefix pool size + per-prefix length
conversation_*                           : multi-turn knobs
mooncake_trace.traces                    : list of {name, path} JSONL traces
mooncake_trace.synthesis_grid            : list of {name, speedup, prefix_len, prefix_root, prompt_len, max_isl, max_osl}
mooncake_trace.fixed_schedule            : true => use trace timestamps verbatim
mooncake_trace.block_size                : KV-cache block size for radix grouping
Customer specific shape: 50K shared (cacheable) prefix +
5K new ISL + 500 OSL at concurrency 32 (one SC16 decode unit).
Two traffic generators model the same shape:
- shared_system : synthetic; shared_system_prompt_length=50000 is sent
as an identical system message across every session
(100% prefix reuse). Exact + deterministic.
- mooncake_trace: trace-driven (AIPerf prefix-synthesis Use Case 3/4);
replays customer_mooncake.jsonl whose 98-block (50K)
root is shared across all sessions. Realistic radix
reuse; scalable via --synthesis-* multipliers.
Either way the steady-state per-session KV-cache hit-rate is
isl_mean=5000 is the *new* per-request input; total ISL/request ~= 55000.
The matched zero-prefix baseline (same 5K ISL / 500 OSL / c32) isolates the
TTFT P50/P90/P99 uplift attributable to prefix caching.
request_count=256 (8 waves of 32) gives usable TTFT percentiles incl. a rough
P99; bump it for a tighter P99. Scrape the worker tt_prefix_cache_* counters
with --prefix-cache-metrics-url to populate the hit-rate column.
goodput enforces the customer SLAs per request: TTFT <= 4000ms (the P50
target as the per-request bar) AND output speed >= 45 tokens/s/user. The
P90<10s / P99<35s / hit>=90% targets are validated from the percentile
columns + the SLA Compliance table. Override with --prefix-cache-goodput.
